description:
You are a data-engineer who has just received a tiny sample feed for the “People → Department” leg of an ETL pipeline.  
Everything you need is already in the directory /home/user/data_pipeline/ :

• /home/user/data_pipeline/employees.json – raw source data.  
• /home/user/data_pipeline/employees_schema.json – a JSON-Schema that the source file must satisfy.

Your assignment consists of three small subtasks, all of which must be completed from a normal Linux shell using nothing more than standard command-line tools (jq, cat, echo, etc.).  You do NOT need any external libraries or root access.

Sub-task 1 – Schema validation  
1. Use jq (or any other CLI tooling available in the container) to make sure that every element of employees.json
   a) is an object,  
   b) contains the keys id, name, department_id, and  
   c) that id and department_id are integers while name is a string.  
2. Write exactly one line of text to /home/user/data_pipeline/validation.log:  
   employees.json: VALID  
   The line must end with a single UNIX newline (LF).  If you decide the data are not valid, replace the word VALID with INVALID.  Nothing else may be written to the file.

Sub-task 2 – Filter employees that belong to department 2  
Create /home/user/data_pipeline/employees_dept2.json that contains ONLY the employees whose department_id is 2.  
• The file must be a JSON array.  
• Preserve the order found in the source file.  
• Pretty-print it with an indent of exactly two spaces.  
• End the file with a single newline.

Sub-task 3 – Produce a department head-count report  
Generate /home/user/data_pipeline/employee_counts.json that summarises how many employees belong to each department_id found in the source file.  
The file must be a single JSON object whose keys are the department_id values converted to strings and whose values are the counts, for example:  
{
  "1": 1,
  "2": 2,
  "3": 1
}
Exactly two-space indentation, keys sorted numerically ascending, and a trailing newline.

Summary of artefacts the automated tests will look for:

1. /home/user/data_pipeline/validation.log  
   • Exactly one line: `employees.json: VALID` or `employees.json: INVALID` followed by a newline.

2. /home/user/data_pipeline/employees_dept2.json  
   • A JSON array containing only the department_id = 2 records, pretty-printed with two-space indent, newline at EOF.

3. /home/user/data_pipeline/employee_counts.json  
   • A JSON object mapping department_id (as string) to count, two-space indent, numerically-sorted keys, newline at EOF.

No other files may be altered or created inside /home/user/data_pipeline/.  Use as few commands as you like (realistically 3-4 commands are enough).  Once all three artefacts meet the exact formatting rules above, the task is complete.
name: task_000000_de186f60
